sat_relevance: Relevant when noble-rot style is explained by alternating humid mornings and dry afternoons.

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Noble rot develops when ripe grapes experience humid or misty mornings followed by warmer, drier afternoons.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Morning humidity allows Botrytis cinerea to establish on the berries, while dry afternoons limit destructive grey rot and promote controlled evaporation through the perforated skins.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The alternating conditions favour beneficial noble rot, producing shrivelled grapes with concentrated sugar, flavour and characteristic botrytised aromas.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Humid mornings initiate Botrytis infection and dry afternoons convert that infection into beneficial noble rot rather than destructive grey rot.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - Phase P.3 Botrytis candidate expansion. Not from official WSET mark scheme.
